name: Luma_Event_Coordinator
description: Provides event management intelligence via Luma's API. Enables the Mind to discover upcoming events, track guest lists, manage calendars, and create events for community coordination and networking.
progressive_unlock: Event listing and details available immediately. Guest management and event creation unlock after Mind has browsed events at least once to establish event context.
constraints: Respect Luma API rate limit of 100 requests per minute
Always validate event_api_id format before making detail requests
Never expose Luma API key in logs, outputs, or error messages
Cache event lists in STREAM with 2-minute TTL to reduce redundant calls
Cache calendar structure in LTM with key 'luma_calendar_{id}' (1-hour TTL)
For guest lists, handle pagination — default page size is 50
When creating events, always validate start_at precedes end_at
Present event times in the user's preferred timezone from DNA.preferences
Track API usage in STREAM.luma_rate_counter for throttle prevention
Treat guest email addresses as PII — never cache in LTM, STREAM only with 60s TTL

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm API IDs are valid strings, dates are ISO 8601, timezones are IANA format.
Retrieve Luma API key from TENET.apiKey.luma. Construct Authorization header as 'Bearer {token}'.

Determine query intent: event browsing (what's happening?), event details (tell me about this event), guest management (who's attending?), calendar overview (show my calendars), or event creation (schedule something new).
For event browsing: execute Luma_ListEvents. Present chronologically with name, date, location, and guest count. Cache in STREAM.luma_events (2min TTL).
For event details: execute Luma_GetEvent with api_id or url. Extract title, description, datetime, location, cover image, and host info. Cache in STREAM.luma_event_{id} (2min TTL).
For guest management: execute Luma_ListGuests with event_api_id. Summarize by approval_status counts. Handle pagination for large events. Cache counts only in STREAM (PII protection).
For calendar overview: execute Luma_GetCalendar then Luma_ListCalendarEvents. Present calendar name and upcoming events. Cache structure in LTM.
For event creation: validate all required fields. Execute Luma_CreateEvent. Confirm success with event URL. Store new event ID in STREAM.luma_created_events.
Compile results with clear formatting. Present dates in human-readable format with timezone context. Include event URLs for direct access.
Log query execution in LTM with key 'luma_query_{timestamp}' including intent, tools used, and rate limit status.
